project.name: rauf
project.summary: A CLI that runs an autonomous coding loop, processing a structured JSON backlog one item at a time with a fresh agent session per item, verified via git commits.

audiences.name: Loop operator
audiences.description: A developer who installs rauf in a project and runs the loop over a backlog.

audiences.name: Supervising agent / CI
audiences.description: A machine consumer driving rauf headless and observing --json / --ndjson output and exit codes.

sources.note: rauf bin entry (shebang bun); dispatches runCli().
runCli() — argv parse and command dispatch.
COMMANDS registry and ExitCode contract (0 SUCCESS … 6 RUNNING).
LoopRunner — per-iteration orchestration; RAUF_DONE/BLOCKED/NEEDS_HUMAN signal parsing.
Canonical Zod schemas: BacklogItem, LoopState, config.
Install -> backlog lifecycle -> status derivation. Integration ground truth.
loop run, agent selection, model override, event formatting.
Existing install + quickstart + CLI overview (mode-mixed: tutorial + reference).
Existing NDJSON event stream + supervisor observation surfaces.

documents.title: Run your first loop
documents.purpose: Take a new user from install to a completed loop over a two-item backlog.

outline.heading: Install rauf
outline.intent: Get the binary on PATH via the npm launcher.

outline.heading: Scaffold a backlog
outline.intent: rauf init / add two items so the loop has work.

outline.heading: Run the loop and watch it finish
outline.intent: loop run to a clean terminal (exit 0).

documents.verification: Install/backlog/loop flow verified against packages/core/src/integration.test.ts. README currently mixes this on-rails tutorial with reference material (mode-mixing) — audit flags it for a single-mode tutorial split.

diagrams.description: The per-iteration loop: read backlog item -> spawn fresh agent session -> parse RAUF_* signal -> commit -> next item. Only the stages verified in packages/loop/src/runner.ts.

documents.title: Recover an interrupted loop
documents.purpose: Resume or reset a loop after an interruption or a blocked item.
documents.verification: state.json fields (status, iteration, currentItem, lastSignal) verified in packages/core/src/schemas.ts.

documents.title: Drive rauf from CI or a supervisor
documents.purpose: Run rauf headless and react to its machine-readable output and exit codes.
documents.verification: Exit-code contract verified in packages/cli/src/commands.ts (ExitCode); event formatting in packages/cli/src/loop-commands.test.ts.

documents.title: CLI command reference
documents.purpose: Look up every command, subcommand, flag, and exit code.

outline.heading: Commands and subcommands
outline.intent: The COMMANDS registry, verbatim and complete.

documents.verification: Command set and flags verified against packages/cli/src/commands.ts; existing docs/SPEC-CLI.md is already single-mode reference and maps 1:1.

documents.title: Backlog schema reference
documents.purpose: Look up every field of a .rauf/backlog.json item.

outline.heading: BacklogItem fields
outline.intent: id, type, priority, acceptanceCriteria, status, dependencies, model, …

documents.verification: Field set verified in packages/core/src/schemas.ts and the generated schemas/backlog.schema.json.

documents.title: How the loop decides an item is done
documents.purpose: Explain the RAUF_DONE / RAUF_BLOCKED / RAUF_NEEDS_HUMAN signal model and why the runner reconciles by git commit.

outline.heading: The three signals
outline.intent: What each signal means and how it is emitted on the final line.

outline.heading: Why commits are the source of truth
outline.intent: The runner reconciles committed work rather than trusting narration.

documents.verification: Signal parsing verified in packages/loop/src/runner.ts. GAP: log-redaction completeness of RAUF_* tokens is asserted in guidance but not covered by a dedicated test — see gaps.

gaps.note: README.md mixes an on-rails getting-started tutorial with CLI reference material (mode-mixing). Audit recommends splitting the tutorial into get-started/your-first-loop and keeping reference in reference/cli.
Web dashboard is documented as a newer, less battle-tested surface than the CLI; no feature-parity matrix exists. Excluded from this end-user plan until stabilized.
Gemini and Cursor agents are documented as argv-verified only (not run-to-completion). Any 'supported agents' claim must reflect that, not assert full parity.
RAUF_* signal-token log redaction is asserted in guidance but not covered by a dedicated test assertion visible in src; do not state redaction is complete as fact.
